name: Dr. Digit's Bavarian Steak with Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the steaks with pepper on both sides and set aside.
2. Sauté the bacon in a skillet until it is cooked through and translucent but not totally crispy. Remove the bacon and set aside.
3. Add 1 tablespoon clarified butter to the bacon fat in the pan, over medium-high heat. Sauté the onion slices until golden brown. Use a slotted spoon to remove the onions and set them aside. Try to extract as many bits of onion as possible, as they can burn if left for the next step.
4. Sauté the steaks about 3 minutes on each side. Sprinkle with salt, add another tablespoon butter to the pan, and sauté an additional minute on each side. Remove the steaks to a heated platter.
5. (Optional) Sprinkle flour into the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, until it becomes slightly browned.
6. Stir in the beef bouillon and scrape up all flavorful brown bits from the bottom of the pan. Strain into a serving bowl.
7. Arrange the steaks on warm plates. Place sauerkraut on one side, covered with strips of bacon, and Spätzle on the other. Pour the pan gravy over the Spätzle, top with fried onions, and serve.
